  options.paths = lib.mapAttrs (_: lib.mkOption) {
      type = lib.types.path;
        Path to the root of the project on which dream2nix operates.
        Must contain the marker file specified by 'paths.projectRootFile'
      type = lib.types.either lib.types.path lib.types.str;
        Path to the directory containing the definition of the current package.
        Relative to 'paths.projectRoot'.
      apply = path': let
        path = toString path';
        if path == projectRoot
        else if lib.hasPrefix projectRoot path
        then lib.path.subpath.normalise "./${lib.removePrefix projectRoot path}"
        else lib.path.subpath.normalise "./${path}";
        Ensure 'paths.projectRoot' contains a file named like this.
        Path to the lock file of the current package.
        Relative to "''${paths.projectRoot}/''${paths.package}"".
        Path to the eval cache file of the current package.
        Relative to "''${paths.projectRoot}/''${paths.package}"".
      type = lib.types.path;
        Absolute path to the lock file of the current package.
        Derived from 'paths.projectRoot', 'paths.package' and 'paths.lockFile'.
      type = lib.types.path;
        Absolute path to the eval cache file of the current package.
        Derived from 'paths.projectRoot', 'paths.package' and 'paths.cacheFile'.

  # ones, if the path dependency isn't in the source dream2nix provides

          from: relPath: ''--replace "\"${from}\"" "\"${sourceRoot}/${relPath}\""''

Match found in: ./09/26/jobs/vendor/nix-task/nix/lib/getTasks.nix
  depsWithDerivations = currentPath: deps:
      else if (isAttrSetWithTaskOutput value) then { __type = "taskOutput"; flakeAttributePath = "${currentPath}.${name}.output"; deps = depsWithDerivations "${currentPath}.${name}.output.deps" value.output.deps; }
  collectMaybeTask = { taskDefinition, currentPath, opts }:
      collectTask { inherit taskDefinition; inherit currentPath; inherit opts; }
      collectTasks { output = taskDefinition.output.deps; currentPath = "${currentPath}.output.deps"; inherit opts; }
      collectTasks { output = taskDefinition; inherit currentPath; inherit opts; }
  collectTask = { taskDefinition, currentPath, opts }:
        flakeAttributePath = currentPath;
        deps = depsWithDerivations "${currentPath}.deps" taskDefinition.deps;
          (map (pathItem: if (hasAttr "drvPath" pathItem) then pathItem.drvPath else pathItem) taskDefinition.path)
        inherit path;
      depsTasks = flatten (mapAttrsToList (key: value: collectMaybeTask { taskDefinition = value; inherit opts; currentPath = "${if currentPath != "" then "${currentPath}." else ""}deps.${key}"; }) taskDefinition.deps);
  collectTasks = { output, currentPath, opts ? {} }:
      (collectTask { taskDefinition = output; inherit currentPath; inherit opts; })
      concatMap (attrName: collectMaybeTask { taskDefinition = output.${attrName}; inherit opts; currentPath = if currentPath != "" then "${currentPath}.${attrName}" else attrName; }) (attrNames output)
  # sort by shortest path to task
  sortTasks = a: b: (builtins.stringLength a.flakeAttributePath) < (builtins.stringLength b.flakeAttributePath);
      currentPath = "";

Match found in: ./09/27/7-concepts/6-qa-testing/tests/2025-01-27-gemini-hello-world/flake.nix

            # 3. Path Resolution Test (from minimal-qa-test)
            echo "=== PATH RESOLUTION TEST ===" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            echo "Attempting to resolve source paths..." | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            # Test different path approaches
            echo "Direct source access:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ Source accessible" || (echo "❌ Source NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
            echo "Bundle path test:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src/bundle" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ Bundle accessible" || (echo "❌ Bundle NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
            echo "Gemini.js path test:" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt
            ls "$src/bundle/gemini.js" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ gemini.js accessible" || (echo "❌ gemini.js NOT accessible" | tee -a $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt && exit 1)
              echo "Source Path: $src"
              echo "Output Path: $out"
            cat $out/diagnostics/path_resolution.txt | tee -a $out/analysis/summary.txt
                "diagnostics/path_resolution.txt"
          # Build and get result path
          RESULT_PATH=$(nix build --no-link --print-out-paths ${self.packages.${system}.default})
          if [ -n "$RESULT_PATH" ] && [ -d "$RESULT_PATH" ]; then
            echo "✅ Test built successfully at: $RESULT_PATH"
            exec "$RESULT_PATH/bin/run-test-report.sh" "$RESULT_PATH"
            echo "❌ Test build failed or result path not found"
Match found in: ./09/27/7-concepts/6-qa-testing/tests/2025-01-27-minimal-qa-test/flake.nix

              echo "Summary not found at: $RESULT_PATH/analysis/summary.txt"
            for file in "$RESULT_PATH/diagnostics"/*.txt; do
            ls -la "$RESULT_PATH/logs/" 2>/dev/null || echo "No logs directory"
            RESULT=$(nix build --no-link --print-out-paths ${self.packages.${system}.default})

Match found in: ./09/crq-012.foaf.nix
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Formal Definition of Nix Pure Derivation in Unimath:** Develop a formal definition of a \"pure derivation\" within the Unimath framework in Lean 4. This definition will capture the essential properties of Nix derivations: content-addressability, immutability, and deterministic output based solely on inputs. This will involve representing Nix store paths, inputs, and outputs as Unimath types. 2.  **Mapping of Nix Properties to Unimath Type Properties:** Systematically map the key properties of Nix pure derivations to corresponding properties of Unimath types: **Reproducibility:** Correlate the deterministic nature of Nix builds to the well-definedness and uniqueness of canonical forms in Unimath types. **Content-Addressability:** Relate the cryptographic hashing of Nix store paths to the identity and equivalence of types in Unimath (e.g., path equality). **Immutability:** Map the immutability of Nix store artifacts to the inherent immutability of mathematical objects and types in Unimath. 3.  **Formal Verification of Derivation Equivalence:** Utilize Lean 4 to formally prove that if two Nix derivations are equivalent (i.e., produce the same output hash), their corresponding Unimath types are also equivalent (e.g., propositionally equal or equivalent via an equivalence). Conversely, prove that distinct Nix derivations correspond to distinct Unimath types. 4.  **Type-Theoretic Representation of Nix Flakes:** Extend the formalization to represent Nix flakes themselves as Unimath types, capturing their structure, inputs, and outputs within the type-theoretic framework. This would allow for formal reasoning about the composition and properties of entire Nix-based projects. 5.  **Implications for Software Verification:** Explore how this formal equivalence can be leveraged for the formal verification of software systems built with Nix. This could involve proving properties about the behavior of Nix derivations directly within Lean 4, providing a new layer of assurance for software correctness.";
Match found in: ./09/verify-foaf-owl.nix

Match found in: ./09/theory/nix_code_indexer.nix
  indexNixFiles = { path, projectRoot, name ? "nix-file-index" }:
      inherit path projectRoot;
      find "${path}" -type f -name "*.nix" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
          relative_path=$(echo "$file" | sed "s|^${projectRoot}/||")
          printf '  {"path": "%s", "hash": "%s"}' "$relative_path" "$file_hash" >> $out/nix-files.index.json

Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/markdown_twin_generator.nix
  # A conceptual function to find and index all .md files in a given path.
    path, # The path to scan for .md files
      inherit path;
      echo "Indexing .md files in ${path}..." >&2
      # Create a JSON array of objects { path, hash }
      find "${path}" -name "*.md" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
        relative_path=$(realpath --relative-to="${path}" "$file")
        echo "  {\"path\": \"${relative_path}\", \"hash\": \"${file_hash}\"}" >> $out/markdown-files.index.json
    markdownFilePath, # Path to the Markdown file in the Nix store
    name ? (builtins.baseNameOf markdownFilePath),
      inherit markdownFilePath;
      echo "Generating HTML for ${markdownFilePath}..." >&2
      pandoc -f markdown -t html ${markdownFilePath} > $out/index.html
      echo "HTML generated for ${markdownFilePath} at $out/index.html" >&2
      # Assume a path to a directory containing Markdown files
      markdownSourcePath = pkgs.lib.cleanSource ./.; # Conceptual: index current directory
      indexedMDFiles = indexMarkdownFiles { path = markdownSourcePath; };
            markdownFilePath = "${markdownSourcePath}/${firstMDFile.path}";
            name = builtins.baseNameOf firstMDFile.path;
Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/n_gram_generator.nix
  # Function to tokenize a file path string into segments and parts.
  tokenizePath = path: 
      pathSegments = lib.strings.splitString "/" (lib.strings.removePrefix "/" (lib.strings.removeSuffix "/" path));
      splitByDot = lib.flatten (lib.map (segment: lib.strings.splitString "." segment) pathSegments);
      path = "/home/user/project/src/my-flake.nix";
      pathTokens = tokenizePath path;
      allTokens = pathTokens ++ nixTokens;
      pathNGrams = generateNGrams { tokens = pathTokens; inherit nGramLengths; };
      inherit pathTokens nixTokens allTokens;
      inherit pathNGrams nixNGrams combinedNGrams;
  inherit tokenizePath;
Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/nix_code_indexer.nix
  # A conceptual function to find and index all .nix files in a given path.
    path, # The path to scan for .nix files
    projectRoot, # The root path to calculate relative paths against
      inherit path projectRoot;
      echo "DEBUG: path = ${path}" >&2
      echo "Indexing .nix files in ${path}..." >&2
      # Create a JSON array of objects { path, hash }
      find "${path}" -type f -name "*.nix" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
          relative_path=$(echo "$file" | sed "s|^$projectRoot/||")
          printf '  {"path": "%s", "hash": "%s"}' "$relative_path" "$file_hash" >> $out/nix-files.index.json
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)
    # The base path from which the index was created, to correctly import files
    basePath, 
    # Create an attribute set where keys are relative paths and values are imported Nix expressions.
        name = fileInfo.path; # Use relative path as attribute name
        value = import "${basePath}/${fileInfo.path}"; # Import the Nix expression
    nixFileIndex, # Output of indexNixFiles (path to nix-files.index.json)

Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/project_nix_path_ngrams.nix
  # A function to generate n-grams from the paths of all Nix files in the project.
  generateProjectNixPathNGrams = { 
    projectRoot, # The root path of the project to index
    name ? "project-nix-path-ngrams",
      path = projectRoot;
    # Extract all file paths
    allNixFilePaths = lib.map (f: f.path) indexedFiles;
    # Tokenize each path and generate n-grams
    allPathNGrams = lib.flatten (lib.map (path: # For each file path
        tokens = nGramGeneratorModule.tokenizePath path;
    ) allNixFilePaths);
      inherit allPathNGrams;
      echo "${lib.strings.concatStringsSep "\n" allPathNGrams}" > $out/nix-path-ngrams.txt
      echo "Generated ${builtins.length allPathNGrams} n-grams from Nix file paths." >&2
  inherit generateProjectNixPathNGrams;

Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/nix_2gram_indexer_step7.nix
    uniquePaths = []; # A deduplicated list of file paths where this 2-gram is found
    pathSetHash = null; # A hash representing the unique set of paths (conceptual prime factorization)
    projectRoot, # The root path of the project to index
        # Get unique file paths where this 2-gram is used
        uniquePaths = lib.unique (lib.map (usage: usage.filePath) usages);
        # Sort paths for consistent hashing of the set
        sortedUniquePaths = lib.sort lib.compareStrings uniquePaths;
        # Hash the sorted unique paths to get a unique identifier for the set
        pathSetHash = builtins.hashString "sha256" (lib.strings.concatStringsSep ";" sortedUniquePaths);
        uniquePaths = sortedUniquePaths;
        inherit pathSetHash;

Match found in: ./10/09/hackathon/nar-bridge-flake/flake.nix
  description = "A flake to bridge between Nix store paths and NAR archives.";
      # Function to create a NAR archive from a given store path
      # Usage: nar-bridge-flake.lib.createNar { name = "my-archive"; path = /nix/store/...; }
      lib.createNar = { name, path }:
          inherit path;
          echo "DEBUG: Running ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix nar pack for path: $path"
          ${pkgs.nix}/bin/nix nar pack "$path" > "$out/${name}.nar" || { echo "ERROR: nix nar pack failed!"; exit 1; }
          echo "$out/restored-content" > "$out/restored-path"
Match found in: ./10/09/hackathon/nar-bridge-flake/test.nix
    path = dummyDerivation;
  restoredPath = narBridgeFlake.lib.restoreNar {
  # 4. Verify the restored path
    inherit restoredPath;
    # Read the actual restored path from the file
    ACTUAL_RESTORED_PATH=$(cat "$restoredPath/restored-path")
    diff -q "${dummyDerivation}/hello.txt" "$ACTUAL_RESTORED_PATH/hello.txt" || exit 1

Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/qa/test-prelude.nix
          # Construct the expected path for the fork (this is a heuristic and might need refinement)
          expectedPath = toString (../../source/github/meta-introspector + "/${miRepo.repo}");
          isPathCheckedOut = builtins.pathExists expectedPath;
          isBranchCorrect = if isPathCheckedOut then
              headContent = builtins.readFile "${expectedPath}/.git/HEAD";
        lib.assertMsg (isPathCheckedOut && isBranchCorrect)
          "Meta-introspector fork '${miRepo.url}' at '${expectedPath}' must be checked out and on branch '${currentBranch}'."

Match found in: ./10/10/context.nix
  # Path to the .gitmodules file for pick-up-nix context
  pickUpNixGitmodulesPath = ../../context/pick-up-nix/.gitmodules;
  # Path to the .gitmodules file for streamofrandom context
  streamOfRandomGitmodulesPath = ../../context/streamofrandom/.gitmodules;
  parseGitmodules = gitmodulesPath:
      content = builtins.readFile gitmodulesPath;
      submoduleSections = lib.strings.match ''^\[submodule "([^"]+)"\]\n\s*path = ([^\n]+)\n\s*url = ([^\n]+)(\n\s*branch = ([^\n]+))?'' content;
          path = lib.head (lib.tail match.captures);
        { inherit name path url branch; };
  pickUpNixSubmodules = if builtins.pathExists pickUpNixGitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules pickUpNixGitmodulesPath else [];
  streamOfRandomSubmodules = if builtins.pathExists streamOfRandomGitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules streamOfRandomGitmodulesPath else [];

    cp -r ${credentialsPath}/* "$HOME/.gemini/"
    echo "✅ Credentials copied from ${credentialsPath} to $HOME/.gemini/"
    # The output of this derivation is the path to the temporary HOME
    echo "$HOME" > $out/home_path

        # The path to the user's actual ~/.gemini directory on the host
        # hostGeminiHome is now configurable via flake inputs or defaults to the hardcoded path
          credentialsPath = hostGeminiHome; # Pass the host's ~/.gemini path

    processEntry = path: name: type:
        fullPath = "${path}/${name}";
        recursiveDefinitions.generate fullPath # Recursive call
        safeEval fullPath # Use the safe-eval template
    generate = path:
        getDirectoryEntries = builtins.readDir path;
        processedEntries = lib.mapAttrs (name: type: recursiveDefinitions.processEntry path name type) getDirectoryEntries;

  extractUrls = { path, name ? "extracted-urls" }:
      inherit path;
        grep -r -o -E "http(s)?://[^\"'<>[:space:]]+" "$path" > "$out"/urls.txt || true

Match found in: ./theory/nix-indexer-pipeline.nix
# from Nix paths and metadata.
  # Defines a simple tokenizePath function and extracts unique terms.
  tokenizePath = path:
      normalizedPath = lib.replaceStrings ["/" "-" "."] [" " " " " "] path;
      tokens = lib.filter (s: s != "") (lib.splitString " " normalizedPath);
  # Extract all tokens from all indexed file paths
  allTokens = lib.flatten (lib.map (file: tokenizePath file.path) indexedFiles);
Match found in: ./wrap_context.nix
{ lib, path, name }:
      parseGitmodules = gitmodulesPath:
          content = builtins.readFile gitmodulesPath;
            (builtins.fromJSON "[ \"^\\[submodule \\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\\]\\n\\\\s*path = ([^\\\\n]+)\\\\n\\\\s*url = ([^\\\\n]+)(?:\\\\n\\\\s*branch = ([^\\\\n]+))?\"]")
              subPath = lib.elemAt match 1;
            { inherit name subPath url branch; };
      # Get .nix files in the current path
      ) (builtins.attrNames (builtins.readDir path));
      # Get submodules in the current path
      gitmodulesPath = path + "/.gitmodules";
      submodulesRaw = if builtins.pathExists gitmodulesPath then parseGitmodules gitmodulesPath else [];
            value = wrap_context { inherit lib; path = path + "/" + sub.subPath; inherit (sub) name; };
      files = map (file: path + "/" + file) nixFiles;
